title: HUF Acceleration & Fix-Point Analysis — Two New Observables
classification: ANALYTICAL — extends the HUF observation framework with two new GOV-safe metrics
citation: Peter Higgins / Rogue Wave Audio, with the HUF AI Collective: Claude (Anthropic), ChatGPT (OpenAI), Copilot (Microsoft), Gemini (Google), Grok (xAI)

origin.peter_hypothesis: Evaluate and consider in the HUF analyzer not just velocity but an acceleration analysis. There might be something useful at the inflection points. Also, once a key point is identified, can a fix variable analysis be useful — fix each variable at that key point one by one and look forward and backward. Acceleration inflection points may be a forward-reverse analysis point of fixation to test between inflections for data conditions of previous unknown construct. Possible hidden dimensional analysis by fix-point study.
test_domains: United Kingdom (EMBER energy — clean transition, full carrier data)

current_huf_observables.K_eff: Effective dimensionality — WHAT is happening to the system's complexity
current_huf_observables.TV_distance: Total variation distance — HOW FAST the system's structure is changing
current_huf_observables.L2_drift: Euclidean drift — complementary velocity metric (per METRIC-001 dual-metric standard)

proposed_new_observables.acceleration: d(TV)/dt — IS THE MOVEMENT SPEEDING UP OR SLOWING DOWN?
proposed_new_observables.fix_point_divergence: Freeze-one-carrier K_eff divergence — WHICH ELEMENT IS DRIVING THE CHANGE?

relationship_to_framework.gov_safety: Both new observables are pure observation. Neither closes the loop. Neither recommends action. Neither produces automated responses. Both comply with LOOP-001 (Open Loop Doctrine).

definition.meaning: The discrete second derivative of structural velocity. Positive acceleration means the system is restructuring faster than the previous period. Negative means it is decelerating.
definition.inflection_point: A time point where acceleration changes sign — the rate of structural change itself is changing direction.

japan_deceptive_drift.regime: Deceptive drift — K_eff declining slowly while TV remains calm, masking the nuclear concentration that preceded Fukushima

key_finding.detail: In the 2005-2010 window, current HUF reads K_eff declining (4.98→4.88) and TV calm (~0.02-0.05). This is the documented deceptive drift pattern. Acceleration adds a new layer: 2010 TV acceleration = -0.031 (decelerating toward stillness), then 2011 flips to +0.102 (massive structural acceleration). The inflection occurs at the year boundary BEFORE the Fukushima event.
key_finding.interpretation: The acceleration trace adds information that velocity alone does not carry. The calm is not just calm — it is a calm that is decelerating toward a minimum, immediately before an explosive acceleration. The inflection point marks the moment the deceptive drift regime reaches its terminal state.

japan_deceptive_drift.verdict: USEFUL — acceleration reveals deceptive drift signature that velocity alone masks

germany_open_transition.regime: Open transition — continuous renewable buildout with explicit policy (Energiewende)

key_finding.description: High inflection density — nearly every other year
key_finding.detail: Germany's energy transition is steady and incremental. The velocity oscillates around an upward trend as renewables grow year over year. This produces frequent sign changes in acceleration — the system is not experiencing regime shifts, it is continuously diversifying. Acceleration inflection points become noise rather than signal.
key_finding.interpretation: In openly diversifying systems, acceleration inflections are a poor discriminator. The system is always accelerating and decelerating in small oscillations around a secular trend. This is the expected behavior: acceleration is most informative where velocity is deceptively calm.

germany_open_transition.verdict: NOISY — acceleration inflections are not discriminative in open transitions

uk_clean_transition.domain: United Kingdom electricity generation (EMBER, 2000-2023, full carrier proportions)
uk_clean_transition.regime: Clean transition — coal collapse followed by renewable replacement

key_finding.description: Inflection points cluster at regime boundaries
key_finding.detail: UK inflection points cluster at key structural transitions: early 2000s (gas growth phase), ~2012 (coal collapse accelerates), ~2016 (transition reaches terminal velocity for coal, renewables take over as primary movers). The inflection points mark the boundaries between distinct structural regimes.
key_finding.interpretation: In clean transitions with distinct phases, acceleration inflection points mark regime boundaries — the moments where the structural driver changes. This is genuinely useful information that velocity alone does not clearly delineate.

uk_clean_transition.verdict: INFORMATIVE — inflection points mark regime phase boundaries

overall_acceleration_assessment.when_useful: Deceptive drift regimes (quiet systems about to break) and clean transitions with distinct phases
overall_acceleration_assessment.when_noisy: Continuous open transitions where diversification is steady and incremental
overall_acceleration_assessment.recommendation: Include as OPTIONAL trace in analyzer display alongside velocity. Highlight inflection points. Do not auto-interpret. Mark as experimental pending further domain validation.

definition.method: At a selected inflection point, freeze one carrier element at its inflection-point proportion value. Redistribute the difference across remaining carriers proportionally to maintain simplex constraint (sum = 1.0). Recompute K_eff forward and backward from the inflection. The carrier whose freezing produces the LARGEST K_eff divergence from reality is the carrier DRIVING the structural change.
definition.what_it_reveals: Which specific carrier element is responsible for the observed structural dynamics at that point in time
definition.what_current_huf_lacks: K_eff and TV treat all carriers equally — they measure the aggregate system state. Fix-point decomposes the aggregate into individual carrier contributions.

early_2000s.interpretation: The structural story is fossil fuel redistribution. Gas is growing at Coal's expense. Wind is emerging but still small. Fix-point correctly identifies Gas as the primary structural driver.

mid_2010s.interpretation: Coal divergence GROWS massively — the coal collapse is the primary structural event. Gas shows the largest absolute divergence because it is the primary beneficiary (absorbing share from coal). Wind begins appearing as a meaningful contributor.

late_2010s_2020s.interpretation: Gas remains the largest single carrier and thus the largest divergence source. Wind and Solar show rising influence — they are becoming the structural drivers as the transition completes. Coal divergence collapses because coal itself has nearly exited the mix.

validation.does_it_match_known_narrative: YES. The UK energy transition narrative is: coal collapsed → gas absorbed temporarily → renewables replaced both. Fix-point analysis recovers this narrative from the mathematics alone, without domain knowledge.
validation.is_it_non_trivial: YES. K_eff alone shows declining complexity then recovery. TV shows velocity spikes. Neither tells you WHICH carrier is responsible. Fix-point does.
validation.is_it_gov_safe: YES. Fix-point produces observation only. It identifies the driving carrier but does not recommend action. The operator reads the fix-point divergence map and decides what it means. LOOP-001 compliant.

overall_fix_point_assessment.when_useful: Any domain where carrier proportions are available and the question is: which element is driving the observed structural change?
overall_fix_point_assessment.when_not_applicable: Domains where only aggregate metrics (K_eff, TV) are available without carrier-level data. Fix-point requires the full proportion vector.
overall_fix_point_assessment.recommendation: Include as OPTIONAL analysis available at any inflection point or user-selected time point. Show as carrier-ranked divergence bar chart. Available when SCHEMA-001 carriers field is present. Do not auto-interpret.

current_huf.observable_2: TV/L2 — HOW FAST it is changing (velocity)

extended_huf.observable_2: TV/L2 — HOW FAST it is changing
extended_huf.observable_3: d(TV)/dt — IS IT ACCELERATING? (rate of rate of change)
extended_huf.observable_4: Fix-point divergence — WHO is driving it (carrier attribution)

combined_framework.relationship: Observables 1-2 are the current HUF instrument panel. Observables 3-4 are optional extensions that add resolution without adding control. The extended framework is strictly backward-compatible: any existing analysis that uses only K_eff and TV continues to work unchanged.

governance_compliance.LOOP-001: COMPLIANT. Both new observables produce readings, not recommendations. The instrument reads. The human decides.
governance_compliance.headline_rule: COMPLIANT. No claims above HSA-001 standing assessment. Acceleration and fix-point are observation tools, not prediction tools.
governance_compliance.skydiver_test: PASS. Both observables show the skydiver additional altimeter readings. Neither pushes the skydiver.
governance_compliance.CLS_boundary: CLEAR. Neither observable crosses into CL-05 territory. No automated responses. No closed-loop feedback. No prescriptive output.
governance_compliance.status: CONDITIONAL — pending collective review and additional domain validation beyond EMBER energy

analyzer_integration.acceleration_panel: New optional trace below the velocity panel. X-axis: time (same as K_eff and TV). Y-axis: d(TV)/dt. Inflection points highlighted with markers. Color: positive acceleration (structural speeding up) vs negative (slowing down).
analyzer_integration.fix_point_panel: Available on click at any inflection point or user-selected time. Shows horizontal bar chart of carrier elements ranked by K_eff divergence when frozen. Forward and backward windows configurable. Requires carriers array in SCHEMA-001 data.
analyzer_integration.backward_compatibility: Both panels HIDDEN when data does not support them. Acceleration hidden if velocity has too few data points (< 4). Fix-point hidden if carriers array absent. No degradation of existing display.

schema_impact.SCHEMA-001: No changes required. Acceleration is computed from existing velocity array. Fix-point is computed from existing carriers array (when present). The schema already accommodates both.
schema_impact.data_requirements: Acceleration: minimum 4 time points with velocity values. Fix-point: carriers array present per SCHEMA-001 optional field.

open_questions.q1: Does acceleration add value in non-energy domains? Need to test against Backblaze (infrastructure), S&P 500 (finance), Planck (cosmology), and ecological templates.
open_questions.q2: Is there an optimal smoothing window for acceleration in noisy data (Germany-type regimes)? A 3-year moving average of TV before differencing might reduce false inflection detection.
open_questions.q3: Can fix-point analysis identify INTERACTING carriers — cases where two carriers move in tandem and neither alone explains the divergence? Current method freezes one at a time; a pairwise freeze might reveal synergistic drivers.
open_questions.q4: Should fix-point divergence be normalized by carrier proportion size? Large carriers will naturally produce larger divergences. A proportion-normalized divergence might better identify small-but-influential carriers.
open_questions.q5: Does the fix-point method work in high-dimensional systems (K_eff > 6)? The UK test used 6 carriers. Planck CMB has more. Computational cost scales linearly with carrier count, but interpretability may degrade.

provenance.hypothesis_source: Peter Higgins, 2026-03-23 late session. Requested pass/fail evaluation of acceleration and fix-point concepts before sleep.
provenance.computation: Three Python scripts executed: acceleration_analysis.py (EMBER AN001 three-country survey), accel_part2.py (Japan EMBER v3 deep dive with 25-year series), fixpoint.py (UK full carrier fix-point analysis with 25 years × 6 carriers × 14 inflection points).
provenance.data_sources: EMBER Electricity AN001, EMBER Deceptive Drift v3.0, UK HDI (case-studies/energy/uk_hdi.json)
provenance.status: CONDITIONAL — results formalized, pending collective review and additional domain testing
